ccel.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

source.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

title: To Queen Brunichild.

source.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Gregory to Brunichild, Queen of the Franks.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: The laudable and God-pleasing goodness of your Excellence is manifested both by your government of your kingdom and by your education of your son.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: To him you have not only with provident solicitude conserved intact the glory of temporal things, but have also seen to the rewards of eternal life, having planted his mind in the root of the true faith with maternal, as became you, and laudable care of his education.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Whence not undeservedly it ensues that he should surpass all the kingdoms of the nations, in that he both worships purely and confesses truly the Creator of these nations.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But that faith may shine forth in him the more laudably in his works, let the words of your exhortation kindle him, to the end that, as royal power shews him lofty among men, so goodness of conduct may make him great before God.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Now inasmuch as past experience in many instances gives us confidence in the Christianity of your Excellence, we beg of you, for the love of Peter, Prince of the apostles, whom we know that you love with your whole heart, that you would cherish with the aid of your patronage our most beloved son the presbyter Candidus, who is the bearer of these presents, together with the little patrimony for the government of which we have sent him, to the end that, strengthened by the favour of your support, he may be able both to manage profitably this little patrimony, which is evidently beneficial towards the expenses of the poor, and also to recover into the possession of this little patrimony anything that may have been taken away from it.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For it is not without increase of your praise that after so long a time a man belonging to Church has been sent for the management of this patrimony.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Let your Excellency, then, deign so willingly to give your attention to what we request of you that the blessed Peter, Prince of the apostles, to whom the power of binding and loosing has been given by the Lord Jesus Christ, may both grant to your Excellence to rejoice here in your offspring, and after courses of many years cause you to be found, absolved from all ills before the face of the eternal Judge.
